phases.name: M10 UI Rendering and Lisp-Driven Themes

steps.name: Alternate screen in terminal guard
criteria: Editor enters alternate screen buffer on startup
Shell history hidden while editor is running
Terminal restored to original state on exit
Alternate screen restored even on panic
Guard handles both raw mode and alternate screen

steps.name: Theme color store on EditorState
criteria: EditorState has a flexible key-value color map
Renderer reads theme colors by name
Default colors match current behavior
Missing theme keys fall back to defaults
Color values support RGB hex and named colors

steps.name: Theme Lisp primitives
criteria: set-theme-color primitive sets a named color
get-theme-color primitive reads a named color
define-theme primitive creates a named theme map
load-theme primitive activates a named theme
Invalid color values return error messages

steps.name: Default theme plugin in Alfred Lisp
criteria: Plugin in plugins/default-theme/init.lisp loads
Sets all standard color slots via Lisp
Status bar has distinct background color
Gutter has distinct foreground color
Removing plugin falls back to terminal defaults
